batches.title: Cooked Quinoa & Grilled Chicken Breast for Mediterranean Lunch
steps: Rinse 300g dried quinoa under cold water
Bring 600ml water to boil, add quinoa, reduce heat to low simmer, cover 15 min until fluffy and water absorbed
Spread on tray, cool to room temperature, portion into 4 containers (100g per serving × 7 days usage)
Pat dry 700g chicken breast fillets with paper towels, season generously with salt, pepper, dried herbs
Preheat grill or large skillet over medium-high heat with 1 tsp olive oil
Grill chicken 6–7 min per side until internal temp 75°C and light golden
Transfer to cutting board, rest 5 min, then slice or cube
Cool slightly, portion 180g (Diego) and 130g (Diana) into separate containers for Monday, store remainder for reuse

day_of_assembly.Monday: Lunch: Combine 100g warm quinoa + 180g warm chicken + 100g roasted Mediterranean veg + lemon & olive oil
day_of_assembly.Tuesday: Lunch: (Different dish — Spiced Turkey Meatballs, no reuse)
day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Lunch: (Different dish — Lean Beef Taco Bowls, no reuse)
day_of_assembly.Thursday: Lunch: (Different dish — Pan-Seared Salmon, no reuse)
day_of_assembly.Friday: Dinner: (Different dish — Asian Stir-Fry, similar chicken base but will use fresh marinade and wok technique)

storage.safety_note: Add a splash of water before reheating to restore moisture. Use within 3 days.

batches.title: Roasted Mediterranean Vegetables & Baked White Fish
steps: Wash 300g zucchini, 200g red/yellow bell pepper, 150g eggplant, 100g cherry tomatoes
Cut zucchini into 1cm semi-moons, dice pepper into 2cm chunks, cube eggplant into 2cm pieces
Toss in a bowl with 2 tsp olive oil, 1 tsp dried oregano, salt, pepper, minced garlic (1 clove)
Spread on large baking tray, roast at 200°C for 25 min until tender and edges caramelised
Cool on tray, portion into containers (100g per serving)
Pat dry 550g white fish fillets (cod, plaice, or hake), season each with salt, pepper, 1 tbsp fresh lemon juice, 0.5 tsp dried herbs
Wrap each fillet individually in parchment or foil with 1 thin lemon slice inside
Stack wrapped fish in a container, refrigerate until day-of
yields: 550g foil-wrapped white fish ready to bake

day_of_assembly.Monday: Dinner: Bake foil-wrapped fish 12 min at 180°C. Serve with 80g warm roasted veg + 100g sweet potato

storage.safety_note: Cooked vegetables store well. Roasted veg can be eaten cold in salads.

batches.title: Spiced Turkey Meatballs & Brown Lentil Salad
steps: In a large bowl, combine 600g lean ground turkey, 50g whole-grain breadcrumbs, 1 egg, 2 tsp Moroccan spice blend (cumin, cinnamon, paprika), 1 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp pepper
Mix gently until just combined (do not overmix)
Using two spoons or ice-cream scoop, portion into 24 balls (~25g each), place on parchment-lined tray
Bake at 200°C for 18 min until internal temp 75°C and no pink inside
Cool on tray, store in airtight container
Rinse 200g dried brown lentils, add to pot with 600ml salted water, bring to boil, reduce to simmer 25 min until tender
Drain, cool slightly, transfer to bowl
Mix in 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 tbsp fresh lemon juice, 1/2 cucumber (diced), 1 diced tomato, salt, pepper, 1 tbsp fresh cilantro
Portion lentil salad into containers (130g and 100g servings)

day_of_assembly.Tuesday: Lunch: Warm 4 (Diego) or 3 (Diana) meatballs. Arrange 130/100g lentil salad on plate, top meatballs, scatter roasted beets

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: Cooked Brown Rice, Seared Tuna Steaks & Blanched Bok Choy
steps: Rinse 180g dried brown rice under cold water
Bring 450ml water to boil, add rice, reduce to low simmer, cover, cook 35 min until tender
Spread on tray to cool and separate grains, portion into containers (80g per serving)
Bring large pot of salted water to boil
Trim 400g bok choy (remove stem base, wash leaves), add to boiling water, blanch 2–3 min until tender-crisp
Drain in colander, spread on kitchen paper to cool and dry, portion into containers
Pat dry 500g fresh tuna steaks (allow to come to room temp 15 min before cooking)
Do NOT cook tuna on Sunday—store sealed in container and sear fresh on the day

day_of_assembly.Tuesday: Dinner: Heat 1 tsp sesame oil. Sear 130/100g tuna 2–3 min per side. Toss 80/60g bok choy with soy + sesame seeds. Plate with warm rice

storage.container: airtight container on ice or coldest shelf
storage.safety_note: Fish spoils fast. Use within 2 days; 1 day is safer. Reheat to 74 °C.

batches.title: Seasoned Lean Ground Beef, Cooked Chickpeas & Brown Rice (Mexican Style)
steps: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat
Add 550g lean ground beef (5% fat), break into small crumbles, cook until no pink remains (~5 min)
Drain excess fat if necessary
Add 2 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp mild chilli powder, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp pepper, 2 minced garlic cloves, 1 tbsp tomato puree
Stir well, cook 2 min more, cool in bowl
Drain and rinse 400g canned chickpeas (or use 150g dried, pre-cooked), toss with beef seasonings as well
Rinse 150g dried long-grain brown rice, bring 375ml water to boil, add rice, cover, simmer 35 min
Cool rice and portion (100g and 75g per serving)
Store beef, chickpeas, and rice separately

day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Lunch: Layer warm rice, seasoned beef, chickpeas. Top with cabbage, tomato, avocado. Drizzle lime + cilantro + olive oil

storage.safety_note: Store in coldest part of fridge. Reheat to 74 °C before serving.

batches.title: Prepared Hummus & Vegetable Sticks
steps: Purchase or prepare 200g high-quality hummus (store-bought is fine for Luxembourg)
Portion into 2 small containers (60g Diego, 50g Diana), store at 4°C
Wash 150g carrots, 100g bell pepper (1/2 large red or yellow), 100g celery
Cut carrots into 7cm batons, dice pepper into 2cm strips, cut celery into 7cm batons
Place vegetables in containers lined with damp paper towels to keep crisp

day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Pm Snack: Spoon hummus into small bowl. Arrange vegetable sticks alongside

storage.safety_note: Cooked beans and lentils keep well. Freeze in portions for quick meals.

batches.title: Herb-Roasted Chicken Thighs & Ratatouille
steps: Pat dry 700g boneless, skinless chicken thighs with paper towels
Season all over with 1 tbsp Herbes de Provence (or dried oregano + thyme + rosemary), salt, pepper
Place skin-side up on lined baking tray, roast at 190°C for 25 min until internal temp 75°C
Cool on tray, portion 120g (Diego) and 90g (Diana) per serving into containers
In a large saucepan, heat 1.5 tsp olive oil over medium heat
Add 150g diced eggplant, 150g diced zucchini, 120g diced red bell pepper, cook 5 min, stirring occasionally
Add 200g diced fresh tomato, 2 minced garlic cloves, 1 tsp dried oregano, salt, pepper, 1 bay leaf
Simmer 20 min until vegetables are tender and sauce is flavourful, cool slightly
Portion ratatouille into containers (100g Diego, 80g Diana per serving)

day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Dinner: Warm 120/90g chicken + 100/80g ratatouille + 80/60g cooked farro. Drizzle olive oil, fresh basil

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: Pan-Seared Salmon & Roasted Root Vegetables
steps: Wash 200g carrot, 150g parsnip, 150g beetroot (beets)
Cut carrots into 2cm chunks, parsnips into 2cm chunks, beetroot into 2cm wedges (leave skin on, wash well)
Toss with 1.5 tsp olive oil, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp pepper on large baking tray
Roast at 210°C for 25–30 min, stirring halfway, until golden and caramelised
Cool on tray, portion into containers (90g Diego, 70g Diana per serving)
Pat dry 550g salmon fillets with paper towels, do NOT cook on Sunday—store sealed and sear fresh Thursday

day_of_assembly.Thursday: Lunch: Heat 1 tsp olive oil. Sear 140/110g salmon skin-side down 4–5 min, flip, cook 2 min. Plate with warm veg + warm barley + lemon & dill

storage.container: airtight container on ice or coldest shelf
storage.safety_note: Fish spoils fast. Use within 2 days; 1 day is safer. Reheat to 74 °C.

batches.title: Marinated Chicken Breast & Stir-Fry Vegetables
steps: Slice 600g chicken breast thinly (5mm) against the grain
In a bowl, combine 1 tbsp low-sodium soy sauce, 1 tsp minced fresh ginger, 1 tsp sesame oil
Add sliced chicken, toss to coat evenly, marinate 30 min (or up to 4 hours refrigerated)
Trim 300g broccoli into small florets, trim 200g snap peas, store separately in containers
Rinse 150g dried brown rice, bring 375ml water to boil, add rice, cover, simmer 35 min
Cool rice and portion (90g Diego, 70g Diana per serving)

day_of_assembly.Thursday: Dinner: Heat 1.5 tsp sesame oil. Stir-fry 100/75g marinated chicken until golden. Stir-fry 80/60g broccoli + 60/45g snap peas 3 min. Add soy sauce, return chicken. Serve over warm rice, garnish sesame seeds & green onion

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: French-Style Green Lentil & Vegetable Soup
steps: Wash and peel 100g carrot, 80g celery, 50g leek (white and light green parts only, no raw onion)
Dice carrot small, celery small, leek into thin half-moons
Heat 1.5 tsp olive oil in a large pot over medium heat
Add carrot, celery, leek, cook without browning 5 min, stirring occasionally
Add 200g dried green lentils (rinsed), 800ml low-sodium vegetable stock, 1 bay leaf, 1 tsp dried thyme
Bring to boil, reduce to simmer, cover, cook 35–40 min until lentils are tender but not mushy
Season with salt and pepper to taste
Cool to room temperature
Portion into containers (350ml Diego, 280ml Diana per serving) — this is a 4-person meal so portions will span 2 days or be split

day_of_assembly.Friday: Lunch: Reheat 350/280ml soup in microwave or stovetop. Pour into bowl, top with fresh parsley. Serve with 30/20g crusty sourdough on side, drizzle olive oil

storage.safety_note: Cooked beans and lentils keep well. Freeze in portions for quick meals.
